page_content: police are one of the most ubiquitous organisations of the society. the policemen, therefore, happen to be the most visible representatives of the government. in an hour of need, danger, crisis and difficulty, when a citizen does not know, what to do and whom to approach, the police station and a policeman happen to be the most appropriate and approachable unit and person for him. the police are expected to be the most accessible, interactive and dynamic organisation of any society. every police officer is supposed to be always on duty and have the powers of a police officer in every part of the state/country.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: powers, duties, role and functions of the police officers in general (psos 88,301 to 304,308,324 & 325)

page_content: in the light of the above mentioned description of police work the following are the important duties and functions of police officers in general.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: powers, duties, role and functions of the police officers in general (psos 88,301 to 304,308,324 & 325)

page_content: 1. crime prevention and preservation of peace and security. 2. crime detection work. 3. investigation related duties and jobs. 4. order maintenance and security jobs. 5. enforcement of social legislation, minor, major and special acts. 6. collection of intelligence. 7. democratic and election related duties. 8. natural calamities, disaster management and emergency duties. 9. maintenance of police records. 10. traffic regulation duties. 11. assistance to other departments. 12. public relations officers duties.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: crime prevention and preservation of peace and security:

page_content: - a) beats and patrolling, including nakabandi, performing picket and ambush jobs, checking of suspected places. - b) surveillance and checking of bad characters. - c) preventive arrests. - d) collection and transmission of criminal intelligence. - e) to take up public awareness programmes.

- a) collection of information/ intelligence about criminals of various types including cybercrime. - b) creation of informers and creating contacts with the members of criminal tribes and other segments of society so as to obtain useful information with regard to the detection of various property and other offences like murder, dacoity, robbery etc. - c) collection of clues properly from the crime scene and by using scientific methods.

- a) crime registration. - b) guarding, protecting, visit to the scene of crime. - c) lifting, handling and packing of exhibits and sending them to required places like the ps, court, fsl, sp office and other places. - d) calling witnesses and serving notices to them. - e) arresting criminals and suspects. - f) search and seizure proceedings during an investigation. - g) interrogation of suspects, criminals and examination of witnesses. - h) collection and recording of various types of evidences i.e. oral, documentary and expert opinion etc. - i) taking criminals to courts for police/ judicial custody and trial. - j) raids for various purposes.

- a) surveillance, watch and action to be taken during peaceful processions, demonstrations and strikes of various types. - b) action to be taken on agitating and unruly mobs. this would include pushing off agitators, stopping them with improvised barricades and effective intervention to contain mobs under the instructions of senior officers and the use of force whenever needed. - c) protection of vital installations during the spate of crimes and emergencies of various types - d) vip security and performance of various duties during vip visits in different capacities.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: enforcement of social legislation, minor, major and special acts:

page_content: - a) to know the significance and importance of various social legislations like child marriage restraint act, protection of civil rights act, anti dowry,

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: enforcement of social legislation, minor, major and special acts:

page_content: guest control and other social legislations which provide a positive and adequate push to social change in a development-oriented society like india.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: enforcement of social legislation, minor, major and special acts:

page_content: - b) to know the significance and importance of various local, special and minor acts along with the role of constabulary in the effective execution of the various provisions thereof.

- a) any incident of law and order - b) political activities - c) labour activities - d) student activities and agitations thereof - e) communal tensions and events - f) employees' associations and strikes by them - g) criminal activities - h) miscellaneous activities and events tending to destroy peace and tranquillity.
in order to perform their role adequately the police must know...

- a) importance of elections - b) types of elections - c) the role of police in ensuring the conduct of free, fair and impartial elections - d) various duties of a constable during different types of elections.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: natural calamities, disaster and emergency duties:

page_content: the police have to attend various jobs like saving life and property, providing shelter, rehabilitation, evacuation of people from crisis situations and their transportation during natural calamities like...

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: natural calamities, disaster and emergency duties:

page_content: - a) fires - b) floods - c) famines - d) the spread of an epidemic - e) the breakout of war or external aggression - f) internal disorders like communal riots, struggle between various classes, castes and sects and other classes.
- a) proper handling of the records - b) upkeep and maintenance of the records - c) preparation, destruction, revision and modification of the records of various police units.

- a) police has to take measures to free flow of the traffic. - b) proper enforcement work shall be taken. - c) traffic awareness meeting shall be conducted. - d) necessary suggestions shall be given to the r&b department for take up road widening and improvements.

- a) the education department during examinations, students, and employees strikes and other situations of disorder, - b) the revenue department and loaning organizations for recovery of loans revenue collection etc., - c) the departments like banks and municipalities for guards etc for the removal of encroachments etc, and - d) the other departments as and when the need arises.

- a) police are the most visible and effective pros of the police department. - b) the police should understand the importance of public relations and its present state in the area of their operations and functions. - c) the officer and the staff must try to improve public relations and police image.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: the following shall be the duties of the assistant sub-inspector.

page_content: 1. investigation of simple cases as directed by sho and assisting investigation in all cases handled by shos and other superior officers. 2. petition enquiry. 3. checking of enquiries in verification rolls. 4. supervision of service of process work. 5. to function as an officer in charge of a beat area and check patrols. 6. direct supervision or handling of station script work. 7. detailing duties to the staff during sub-inspectors absence and exercising supervision and control over the constables and head constables. 8. maintenance of cash book. 9. preparation of pay and travelling allowance bills (he should sign them only when the si is absent). 10. maintenance of arms and ammunition and registers connected there- with. 11. to be in charge of out-post or beat and perform these duties when posted there. 12. such other work as may be entrusted to him.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties of the general duty head constable:

page_content: head constables are mainly employed to be in charge of general duty in police stations, as station writer, as officer-in-charge of out-post and guards, armed reserves, in charge of beat areas in rural and town police stations. he acts as sho in the absence of sub-inspector and asst. sub-inspector. he is authorised to hold inquests and make investigation when asked by the sub inspector to do so.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties of the general duty head constable:

page_content: - a) he will work with constables and help them to understand instructions, catechism and drill and help them. - b) to perform duties allotted by sho. - c) he shall be in charge of guard or escort when deputed. - d) he shall visit villages in the station jurisdiction when deputed for a specific purpose. - e) to work as hc in charge of a particular beat area.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties of the general duty head constable:

page_content: - f) he will attend to court work under the orders of sho. - g) to investigate cases when deputed by the sho and to assist the io in investigation. - h) to conduct enquiries into petty complaints. - i) to take care of arrested persons kept in police station. - j) he will take care of reception and proper behaviour with the persons coming to police station and to attend telephone calls. - k) to be present in the police station in the absence of sub-inspector unless called away in an emergency. he should take approval of s.i before leaving the station on routine duty.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: c) duties of the head constable as station writer: (pso-108 )

page_content: 1. head constable shall normally be assigned the duties of station writer. he will perform the ministerial work of the station under the direction of sho. 2. in the absence of si or asi, he will allocate urgent duties to the constables and be in charge of station property including arms and ammunition and carry out the routine work of the station. 3. in the absence of the si, asi and other senior, hc, he will function as station house officer; take steps for registration and investigation of cases reported under his charge. 4. he may also be employed by sho to go on night rounds and may occasionally be kept in charge of beat area. 5. he is also responsible to watch the arrested persons kept in police station and proper reception to persons visiting police station and also for answering telephone calls when sho, si, or asi are not present in police station.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: d) duties of head constable as investigation assistant :

page_content: the head constables form the main strength of investigating team. they assist the team leader in the investigation of cases especially in preserving, and collecting the clues like finger prints, foot prints, materials, photograph etc. and also in...

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: d) duties of head constable as investigation assistant :

page_content: 1. collection of information, 2. process service, 3. pursuit of clues and arrest of accused and recovery of property, 4. assisting in searches and seizures, 5. surveillance of specially marked persons,

this registry is kept alphabetically at the rural, town, and city police stations, and is organized by village, locality, and street. this registry should contain the names of all the people listed in parts iii and gcr. some pages might be set aside for entering the names of offenders who live beyond the boundaries of this police station but operate within its jurisdiction, or a separate police station wise ex-convict register could be kept. the ex-convicts should be reviewed by the sub- inspector every half year, and the current actions should be logged against them, along with the date of the inspection. ex-convict check memos should be sent to the concerned police stations of those ex-convicts who live in other ps limits. after receiving their responses, make the necessary entries against each.

a register in form 113 should be maintained at the police station to check the movements of the kds & suspects. a separate register should also be maintained at the police outpost. the names of the notorious shall also be entered in this register. the names of kds followed by those of suspects will be written beat-wise. the entries against each person should be filled in, on the basis of reports received from beat constables or of facts ascertained through personal inquiries by the sho. the presence or absence of the person will be noted in the register by means of the symbol 'p' or 'a' under the date with hour of check and number of constable or the designation of the officer who checked.

the names of criminals and suspects who are addicted to the commission of crime at night should be entered in 'red ink' and should be checked during the night. the names of others should be in 'blue ink'. inquiries should be made about all the criminals during the day.
associates of 'night criminals', though not known to have themselves operated during the night, should also be shown in 'red ink' and checked during the night.

when a person whose name was entered in gcr part-iii, particularly the history sheeted person, moves or changes his place of residence to the limits of another ps, the fact shall be communicated to that ps by means of bad character roll form "a" (form 109) for entry in the register of that system. the roll will be returned with the page number of the register on which the entry has been made, noted thereon. a report about the presence or absence and activities of the bc particularly when he becomes a temporary resident within the limits of another ps, his name should be entered by the latter ps in the kd check register. the roll shall be retained until the individual moves on when it will be forwarded to the station of his destination.
in the case of a criminal who is known to have gone to another state,
b.c. roll form 'a' shall be sent directly to the sho in the other state.

when a 'stranger' of suspicious conduct or demeanor is found within the limits of a police station, the sho shall forward a bad character roll form 'b' (form no. 116) to the ps in which the 'stranger' allegedly resides for immediate verification and report.

if the 'stranger' states that he resides in another state, bc roll form 'b' shall be sent directly to the station house officer of the station in the other state for verification and report. the receipt of such rolls should be immediately acknowledged, necessary verification should be done and a report sent to the concerned.

all property offences should be considered professional. in every property offence, soon after registration, the sho should send crime card-i in form no.108 to the district crime records bureau. brief facts of the case with details of mo stolen property, offenders, etc., should be carefully filled up by the sho.

after registration of the offence the investigating officer should visit the scene of offence, collect further information on the crime and the criminals and send the additional facts through (another) crime card-ii dispatching from the scene directly to the district crime records bureau.

the staff of the district crime records bureau, on receipt of the crime cards-i&ii, one and two will check the various indices maintained by it, and see whether any criminal of the same mo has operated in the area previously or has any relations or associates in the station limits or the neighboring station or any criminal who was recently released from jail, is likely to be suspected, and send the advisory memo, to the io.

when a bc absents himself or goes out of view, whether wanted in a case or not, the information should be disseminated in form 117 to the police stations having jurisdiction over the places likely to be visited by him and also to the neighbouring stations, whether within this state or outside. copies of the card should be sent by the sho to the sdpo and to the fpb, if his finger prints are on record and to dcrb. if he is traced and intimation is received of his arrest or otherwise arrangements to get a complete and true picture of his activities should be made and the concerned record updated.

the crime abstract should show the total no. of cases registered under various sections of law, their disposal eg: investigation refused, mistake of law, mistake of fact, civil nature, cases under investigation, charged, pending trial, convicted, acquitted, etc. it should also mention the number of accused persons arrested, charged, etc., and property lost and recovered.

the law and order stations must prepare full crime abstract obtaining the results of the cases from the crime branch. the central crime station must maintain a separate crime abstract in respect of the crimes actually handled by it.

the abstract for the first quarter will contain cases reported during that quarter, and the abstract for the second quarter will include cases reported during the first quarter. the abstract for the third quarter will include, cases of the two previous quarters, and that for the fourth will contain all the cases reported during the year.
the abstract should show the actual state of things on the last day of the quarter.

all cases of previous years un-disposed at the beginning of the year will be shown in a separate abstract at the head of the abstract for the current year until they are disposed of, their disposal being shown in the quarter in which it occurs.
when offence falls under more than one section, it must be shown under the major section.

abetments punishable under chapter iv of bns. other than those specified in sections 55 to 60 of bns should be shown with the substantive offence abetted. attempts should be shown immediately after the offence was attempted.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: 9) finger print slips and conviction memorandum:

page_content: finger print bureau (fpb) maintains the record of the finger prints of the criminals. there is a finger print bureau for the state and there is also a central finger print bureau (cfpb) located in calcutta.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger prints are of two types

page_content: - 1) plain finger prints - 2) rolled finger

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: - 1) finger print record slip - 2) finger print search slip.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: 'fp record slip' of a person is prepared in form no.146 immediately after he is 'convicted' and sent for the record to fpb., hyderabad along with conviction memo (form. no.135), whereas fp search slip is the finger print slip of a 'suspect' taken on form no.146 by the sho and sent to fpb, hyderabad with form no. 147 to find out his 'antecedents'.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: care should be taken in preparing the fp slips. inking of the fingers should be done carefully and the plain and rolled prints taken on the fingerprint slips and ensured that the impressions are clear. immediately after this, the name, residence, and other particulars including details of conviction should be recorded in the slip. finger print slips of one person should be prepared and completely filled in before taking up the other persons in order to avoid confusion.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: if a finger is missing or deformed and an impression cannot be obtained, this fact should be mentioned in the space allotted for that finger. in the case of double fingers, the prints of both the fingers should, if possible, be taken or otherwise, the print of the more prominent of the two be taken. if a person has more than five fingers, and if the extra finger is outside the thumb, its impression should be taken in the space on the slip before the thumb and in case it is outside the little finger, in the space after the little finger.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: finger prints of 'lepers' are not to be taken into any account. persons suffering from 'contagious or infectious' diseases should not be finger printed until they are completely recovered.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: as per section 3 of the criminal procedure (identification) act, 2022-

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: any person, who has been, -

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: - a) convicted of an offence punishable under any law for the time being in force; or - b) ordered to give security for his good behaviour or maintaining peace under section136 of bnss for a proceeding under section 126 or section 127 or section 128 or section 129 of bnss or - c) arrested in connection with an offence punishable under any law for the time being in force or detained under any preventive detention law, shall, if so required, allow his measurement to be taken by a police officer or a prison officer in such manner as may be prescribed by the central government or the state government:

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: provided that any person arrested for an offence committed under any law for the time being in force (except for an offence committed against a woman or a child or for any offence punishable with imprisonment for a period not less than seven years) may not be obliged to allow taking of his biological samples under the provisions of this section.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: finger print slips are of two types

page_content: "measurements" includes finger-impressions, palm-print impressions, footprint impressions, photographs, iris and retina scan, physical, biological samples and their analysis, behavioural attributes including signatures, handwriting or any other examination referred to in section 51 or section 52 of bnss.
the police should maintain close relation/co-ordination with the following govt. departments for successful/smooth function of policing.

executive magistrates: in every mandal, tahsildar is the executive magistrate. the maintenance of law and order in their jurisdiction is the primary duty. police will co-operate for implementation of govt. welfare schemes and maintenance of law and order. the executive magistrates will exercise their powers as magistrates, in cases of maintaining public peace and tranquility. they will conduct enquiries and inquests in custodial deaths (lockup deaths). they will conduct exhumation of dead bodies, if they are buried, without proper investigation, in case of unnatural deaths.

pahani or adangal adangal also known as pahani. in telangana it called as pahani in andhra it called adangal. it is an important revenue record which has particulars relating to lands such as the owners' details, area assessment, water rate, soil type, nature of possession of the land, liabilities, tenancy
patta: transactions involving the land in question require a patta. it can also be used to establish the right of ownership in a court of law.

access to justice is fundamental to the 'rule of law'. if the citizens feel that access to justice is delayed or is not effective, they get alienated. if they feel that punishment is meted out to the wrong doers only in a few cases, they have a tendency to disassociate them with the judicial process and stop taking interest as a complainant, a witness or a panch witness.

sometimes a landlord looking to get a recalcitrant tenant out, a tenant seeking protection from an avaricious landlord, families involved in property disputes, start by thinking that gangsters (goondas) can settle their problems which the courts are going to take ages to do. if this becomes common, criminality will be a substitute for 'rule of law'. we have to ensure that we do not reach such a precarious situation. criminals should be intimidated by what shakespeare called "the awful majesty of the law", otherwise they will become a law unto themselves. various law commissions have suggested reforms in the criminal and civil justice administration system, which need to be acted upon.

the police officers has to have cordial relations with the prosecuting officers. even though the prosecuting officers has no duty to take part in the investigation as per the law, it is suggested that the police officers must have liaison with the prosecuting officers not only in getting conviction but also from the beginning of the investigation i.e. from taking the report/ petition from the complainant and in doing investigation of important cases for proper investigation and to get the accused to the court effectively and to get conviction for prevention of the crime and to preserve peace and tranquility in the area.

the designated medical officer is an 'expert ' as per law. the evidence of medical officer is very important in the courts. medical officer will examine and treat the injured persons, sent by police, in medico legal cases. he will conduct autopsy over the dead bodies, sent by police and issue wound certificates and post mortem reports, respectively. the evidence of doctor is very essential for all bodily offences.

the state excise department is one of the major income departments. it is earning large portion of the income, to the state government. the collection of taxes, through excise and prevention of illicit liquor and related offences, are the duties of excise department. as the civil police are also given powers to prevent the offences under the excise act, it will extend co-operation to the excise department in its functioning.

the transport department is also an important source of earning for the state govt. registration of all the public vehicles plying in the state and collection of taxes, are the important duties of this department. persons, who drive vehicles, must possess a driving license and it should be issued by the transport department, after conducting prescribed tests to the applicant. the transport department should ensure that all the owners of vehicles possess their valid documents. as police department is also vested with powers under the motor vehicle act, curbing of illegal practices, while using the vehicles, is also the responsibility of police. police must extend co-operation to the transport department, whenever it seeks assistance. proper security must be provided to the transport department, if the illicit vehicles are handed over for safe custody.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: g) woman and child welfare department.

page_content: police coordination with woman & child welfare officials while investigating the crime against women and children, is very important. some examples are:

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: g) woman and child welfare department.

page_content: - a) abandonment of child under twelve years of age by parent (offence u/s 93 of bns), for temporary custody. - b) rescue, rehabilitation and re-integration of women and child victims of trafficking cases.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: g) woman and child welfare department.

page_content: - c) mentally retarded/ unknown women and children traced in 'missing person' cases, for custody pending investigation. - d) recovery of infants in illegal adoption cases and missing infants from hospitals, plays crucial role in taking care of the infants.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: g) woman and child welfare department.

page_content: any other cases in which custody/ counseling/ assistance in the investigation of crime against women and children, the role of women and child welfare officials is required.

the education department is also an important wing of the state. the increase in the literacy will improve the standards of public. higher education, vocational courses, will improve the standard of living, among the public. poverty and unemployment are the main reasons for the law and order problems. police must properly assist the education department, in conducting examinations. police must maintain rapport with education department, to prevent the offences relating to examinations, ragging and eve teasing etc.

the municipal department is also an important department. it is the essential for the public to day to day life. the department maintains the sanitation in town and collects the waste material from the town and throw out of the town. it provides the drinking water supply to the towns. it regulates the new constructions of buildings and roads. during the regulation sometimes law and order problem may occurs police will assist the municipal authorities whenever needed. municipal authorities will provide the basic infrastructure like traffic signals, road dividers, painting the roads etc., for better traffic management.

the r and b department is normally responsible for preparation of helipad, barricading and stage arrangements in the event of visit of vips, official meetings and functions. the scenes of collapse of stages/barricading are seen many times which are considered to be severe security lapse on part of police. laying of roads during vip visit is sometimes taken up in the last hour ignoring the quality norms. it is also responsible for the arrangements for the accommodation of vips. police shall have coordination with r & b officials in above issues according to the security norms.
co-ordination with fire service is required in fire accidents, vip visits and tours fairs and festivals and rescue operations during natural calamities.

the rainfall of the country depends upon the area of forest. the protection of forests is very important. protection of wild life in the forest, along with other species is an important issue for the maintenance of ecological balance of the environment. the protection of forest is the duty of forest department. police will assist the forest department, in checking unauthorized persons. prevention of unauthorized cutting of trees and hunting of wild animals. police will also assist the forest department in the service of summons and execution of warrants, as and when required, by the forest department.

electricity department plays a key role in the society. this department is catering to the needs of public. sufficient powers require to be supplied to the industries, agriculture and for domestic needs, basing on demand. the progress of the country can be achieved on war footing. the prosperity of the state is depending upon the supply of power. police department must give proper and required assistance to the electricity department, while checking power thefts and misuse of electricity, by the people who are not covered under the scheme of free power supply.

police coordination with banks is crucial for detecting, investigating, and preventing financial crimes, particularly cybercrimes. this collaboration involves sharing information, coordinating investigations, and working together to freeze fraudulent accounts and recover stolen funds.

banks share suspicious transaction reports (strs) and other relevant information with law enforcement agencies like the indian cybercrime coordination centre (14c). this helps identify potential cybercrime activities and assist in investigations.
